name: Alltru
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Greater St. Louis with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards. Not-for-profit cooperative offering higher savings rates and lower loan rates to 40K+ members.
description_long:
Alltru Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative based in St. Louis that has served the community for 58 years. Founded on cooperative principles, Alltru operates as a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) with a stated mission to provide accessible banking to Greater St. Louis residents. The organization positions itself around membership rather than customer relationships, emphasizing member ownership and direct access to leadership.

alltru offers a comprehensive range of consumer financial products including personal and business checking accounts, multiple savings options (Primary Savings, High Yield Online Savings, Money Market, CDs, IRAs), auto loans, home loans, personal loans, credit builder loans, lines of credit, specialty loans (RV/Boat/Motorcycle/Golf Cart), and Visa credit cards in multiple tiers (Rewards, Low Rate, Secured). They provide additional services like debt protection, auto loan protection, GAP protection, and investment planning services. The credit union also offers youth-focused accounts (TruSpend Checking and Savings for ages 8-17) and children's savings accounts.

Alltru's primary differentiators center on its not-for-profit structure, CDFI designation, and community focus. As a member-owned cooperative, members theoretically have ownership stakes and input into operations. The institution emphasizes higher interest rates on savings and lower rates on loans compared to traditional banks, leveraging its non-profit status. They highlight community reinvestment (53,000 volunteer hours since 2013), free access to 35,000 ATMs and 5,600 shared branches through credit union networks, and personalized service. Current promotions include $300 bonuses for opening checking accounts and 90-day no-payment periods on auto loans.

While Alltru presents genuine advantages for St. Louis area members seeking community-focused banking with competitive rates, the organization has geographic limitations—membership and full service access is restricted to the Greater St. Louis metro area. The website emphasizes relationship banking and community values but provides limited specific rate comparisons or product details to enable direct comparison with national banks or other credit unions. Like most credit unions, membership eligibility requirements apply, though the CDFI status suggests inclusive membership policies. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (standard and premium tiers)
Youth checking and savings accounts (TruSpend, ages 8-17)
Multiple personal savings products (Primary Savings, RollUp Savings, High Yield Online Savings, Money Market)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Auto loans with 90-day no-payment promotional option
Personal loans and credit builder loans
Specialty loans (RV, boat, motorcycle, golf cart)
Visa credit cards (Rewards, Low Rate, Secured options)
Business loans and business savings products
Debt protection, auto loan protection, and GAP protection products
Financial education resources (Learning Center, Zogo gaming, calculators)
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure provides ownership stakes and theoretical governance input, differentiating from customer-only bank models
CDFI certification demonstrates commitment to community financial access and local reinvestment ($53,000+ volunteer hours documented)
Not-for-profit status enables competitive rates: advertises higher savings rates and lower loan rates than traditional banks
Extensive ATM and branch access through 35,000 free ATMs and 5,600 shared branches via credit union networks
Diverse loan products including specialized options (90-day no-payment auto loans, credit builder loans, RV/boat financing)
Youth financial education programs (TruSpend accounts for ages 8-17, Zogo gaming financial literacy tool)
$300 new account bonus for adult checking accounts demonstrates member acquisition commitment
cons: Geographic restriction to Greater St. Louis metro area limits membership eligibility and convenience for customers outside region
Website provides minimal specific rate information, making direct APR/APY comparisons with competitors difficult without direct inquiry
Limited transparency on membership eligibility criteria despite CDFI status suggesting inclusive mission
Shared branch/ATM network reliance means less control over service consistency compared to banks with owned branch networks
No mention of digital-only or fully remote account opening/service options, suggesting potential friction for non-local or mobile-first customers
best_for: Greater St. Louis residents prioritizing community-focused banking and local reinvestment over national brand convenience
Credit union members seeking competitive rates on auto loans and savings products with personalized service
Individuals building credit through credit builder loans who value educational support and relationship banking
Families wanting youth financial literacy tools and accounts designed for children's financial development
diagnosis: Alltru is best for Greater St. Louis residents who value community-focused banking, member ownership, and competitive not-for-profit rates over national bank convenience. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to the St. Louis metro area—membership benefits and service access are unavailable to customers outside this region, making it unsuitable for those requiring nationwide branch access or remote-only services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
